title: Division 10 Bid Reasoning & Install Intelligence
purpose: A deeper reasoning layer for commercial Division 10 intake, scope interpretation, bid building, hidden-scope detection, and labor-operation inference.
design_principles: Understand document meaning before mapping products.
Separate scope from commercial terms, pricing boilerplate, and proposal metadata.
Infer work operations from construction language, not just SKU matches.
Flag hidden scope, assumptions, and likely exclusions so the app can reason like an estimator.

block_types.parser_action: ignore_for_scope; retain for document metadata only

block_types.description: Project name, proposal date, plans dated, addenda, acceptance timing.
block_types.parser_action: store as proposal metadata; do not create labor or material lines

block_types.description: Manufacturer or category header that introduces scope items.
A line that contains quantity and product description/model.
block_types.parser_action: extract as item line with product attributes

block_types.description: An alternate material or finish option, often with separate pricing.
block_types.parser_action: capture as alternate pricing / material option, not duplicate base scope

block_types.description: Material subtotal or category price line.
block_types.parser_action: store pricing metadata; never treat as item scope

examples: IF LABOR IS NEEDED, PLEASE CALL FOR QUOTE
BASE BID PER SPECS AND PLANS
block_types.parser_action: route to commercial assumptions / exclusions / logistics; do not create scope items

block_types.description: A short phrase that indicates included accessories or hardware.
block_types.parser_action: attach to current item/group as inclusion or spec qualifier

commercial_term_patterns: BASE BID PER SPECS AND PLANS
reasoning_rules: If a line is mostly identity/contact text, classify as non-scope.
If a line contains quantity plus model/description, classify as scope_item.
If a line contains price only and no install noun, treat as subtotal/option.
If a line contains tax, bond, unload, shipment, or bid language, route to commercial_terms.
If a line contains material family + price and is adjacent to another family + price, treat as alternates.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Normalize document
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Clean headers/footers, merge broken OCR lines, keep nearby context.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Classify blocks
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Separate scope lines, option lines, metadata, pricing lines, and commercial terms.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Build scope groups
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Attach items to the nearest manufacturer/category header and preserve options.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Interpret construction meaning
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Map not just products but work operations, install dependencies, and code-sensitive conditions.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Detect hidden scope
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Look for common Division 10 phrases that imply extra prep, carpentry, backing, anchoring, layout, or field verification.

bid_reasoning_flow.name: Build bid package
bid_reasoning_flow.machine_thought: Separate material pricing, labor pricing, logistics, tax, bond, exclusions, alternates, and clarifications.

construction_semantics.meaning: This is not a generic locker set. It is a separate carpentry/prep operation before locker placement.
implied_operations: measure locker run and base depth
anchor base to wall/floor as applicable
install plywood or runner decking if base type requires
construction_semantics.when_to_add_labor: Always add separate labor before locker installation unless proposal explicitly says base by others.
common_assumptions: Base height and toe-kick detail are shown on drawings.
Locker dimensions are known before base fabrication.
risk_flags: If base detail is missing, add field verify / detail-required flag.
If existing slab is uneven, elevate leveling labor.
If finish carpentry appearance matters, do not bury labor under generic locker minutes.

construction_semantics.meaning: Partition style determines anchoring, hardware, alignment, and install sequence.
implied_operations: verify wall backing at required points
hang doors and adjust hardware for true openings
construction_semantics.when_to_add_labor: Whenever partition mounting type is specified; labor differs from ceiling-hung and floor-to-ceiling conditions.
risk_flags: Uneven floor or out-of-plumb walls increase install difficulty.
Accessible stall geometry may require more layout verification than stall count suggests.

construction_semantics.meaning: Install is code-sensitive and often backing-sensitive; not just quantity times SKU.
construction_semantics.when_to_add_labor: Always; elevate further for existing finished restroom, tile/masonry substrate, or unknown backing.
common_assumptions: Blocking/backing exists or is by others unless priced.
Heights and locations come from drawings or field verification.
risk_flags: Conflicts with dispenser clearances or grab bar position

construction_semantics.meaning: Cabinet type affects projection compliance and wall-condition scope.
implied_operations: check wall condition and recess requirement
review protrusion risk in path of travel
construction_semantics.when_to_add_labor: Whenever cabinet type appears; separate cut-in/recess framing from surface mount logic.
common_assumptions: Location is approved by drawings/code review
risk_flags: Surface-mounted cabinet may violate projection constraints in circulation path.
Recessed units may require framing/cut-in by others unless included.

general_knowledge: Grab bars are structural accessories, not finish trim.
Backing or reinforcement is a core install dependency.
ADA location and accessory clearance can matter.
proposal_assumptions_default: Adequate backing by others unless specifically included.
Final mounting locations per approved drawings/field verification.

general_knowledge: Wall-supported accessory with significant load and mounting sensitivity.
Existing-wall substrate and reinforcement can materially affect install.
hidden_scope: wall repair or reinforcement by others unless priced
proposal_assumptions_default: Suitable wall support exists or is by others.

general_knowledge: Often lower complexity than grab bars but still substrate-sensitive and layout-sensitive.
ADA height and edge conditions can matter depending on mirror type/location.
proposal_assumptions_default: Suitable wall surface ready to receive accessories.

general_knowledge: Backing, floor anchors, plumb/level, and hardware adjustment are integral.
proposal_assumptions_default: Layout dimensions and mounting conditions per approved drawings.
Adequate backing exists where required unless explicitly included.

general_knowledge: Locker runs are systems, not just individual boxes.
Filler/end/corner conditions and anchoring are common hidden labor.
proposal_assumptions_default: Layout dimensions and fillers per drawings.

general_knowledge: Extinguishers and cabinets are not the same install condition.
Cabinet projection and mounting condition can create ADA/path-of-travel issues.
proposal_assumptions_default: Approved mounting locations by others unless field verification included.

general_knowledge: Location is part of compliance, not an afterthought.
Signs can fail inspection even when neatly mounted.
proposal_assumptions_default: Approved sign schedule and mounting locations provided.

term_meanings.app_behavior: Treat as pricing rule / tax toggle.

term_meanings.meaning: Commercial requirement flag, not labor scope.
term_meanings.app_behavior: Store as bond toggle; never parse as item.

term_meanings.term: IF LABOR IS NEEDED, PLEASE CALL FOR QUOTE
term_meanings.meaning: This proposal is material-only or incomplete scope for labor.
term_meanings.app_behavior: Set proposal_scope_type = material_only unless contradicted elsewhere.
Store in logistics; do not price unload labor unless scope says otherwise.

term_meanings.meaning: Field verification was excluded from base price.
term_meanings.app_behavior: Flag uncertainty for dimensions / substrate / unknown conditions.

term_meanings.term: BASE BID PER SPECS AND PLANS
term_meanings.meaning: Generic compliance statement; too broad to create scope by itself.

add_operations: allow for slow drilling / finish protection
hidden_scope_recipes.assumption_if_not_priced: Existing walls are ready and adequately reinforced where required.
Fillers/end panels per manufacturer layout are included only if clearly shown.
Walls/floors are true enough for standard install.

add_operations: separate cut-in/framing by others vs included work
hidden_scope_recipes.assumption_if_not_priced: Opening/framing by others unless proposal expressly includes it.

example_reasoning_from_uploaded_files.observed_pattern: Header metadata + material groups + material subtotals + commercial qualifiers
what_should_not_become_scope: base bid per specs and plans
estimator_thought: This appears material-oriented, not a full install quote, because it literally says labor should be quoted separately.
Semi-recessed cabinet line carries location/projection logic but not enough info to assume recess framing is included.

example_reasoning_from_uploaded_files.observed_pattern: Takeoff names the products; proposal compresses them into grouped pricing and adds alternate partition material options.
what_hidden_scope_is_likely: Partition mounting and adjustment labor driven by overhead-braced floor-mounted condition
Potential ADA stall/layout sensitivity not visible from stall count alone
estimator_thought: The takeoff is useful for product count, but the proposal reveals commercial assumptions and alternates.
14 stalls does not fully describe labor; mounting type and field condition matter.

self_questions_for_reasoning_engine: Is this line a product/scope line, or a commercial/metadata line?
Does the phrase imply a separate operation before or after the main install?
Is the document material-only, install-only, or both?
Are there code-sensitive items where location matters, not just quantity?
Did the proposal exclude site visit, unload, or labor, which increases uncertainty?
Does a mounting style or base condition imply carpentry, anchoring, layout, or shimming labor?
